pain_point_label: Policy Inconsistency and Version Control Across Campuses

matches.match_rationale: Policies scattered across Word docs, PDFs, and campus intranets are semi-structured but inconsistent — different versions, schemas, and naming conventions across campuses. AI can ingest this corpus and normalize policies into a canonical structured format (version, campus, effective date, category, content sections), making drift immediately visible. This directly addresses the control-weakness root cause: unstructured, incomparable policy documents.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI pipeline that ingests policy documents from all campus drives, extracts metadata (version, date, scope), maps them to a unified schema, and flags version mismatches across campuses — feeding a central policy registry.

matches.match_rationale: Once policies are standardized, AI can continuously monitor the corpus for version drift — detecting when a campus document deviates from the approved master policy in content, date, or structure. This is precisely an anomaly-detection task: flagging documents that deviate significantly from the expected/current standard, not categorizing them into predefined labels. This closes the audit gap where inspectors find policy drift and flag it as a control weakness.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A monitoring agent that runs weekly comparisons of all campus policy documents against the master policy repository, generating an anomaly report listing campuses with out-of-date or modified policies for compliance officer review.

matches.match_rationale: During a grievance or audit, staff and compliance officers need authoritative answers to questions like 'What is the current discipline policy for Campus B?' across a fragmented document landscape. RAG grounds answers in the verified, current policy corpus and provides citations — enabling staff to query policy intent without browsing shared drives, and ensuring answers reflect the approved version rather than a stale local copy.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A policy Q&A assistant that retrieves answers from the central, version-controlled policy repository with citations, replacing ad-hoc drive searches and ensuring all campuses reference the same authoritative source.

matches.match_rationale: When policies need updating — due to regulatory changes, new DepEd directives, or accreditation feedback — AI can draft revised policy language from a brief spec, maintaining consistent voice and structure across all policy types. This reduces the friction of centralized policy authorship, making it practical for a small compliance team to manage a large multi-campus policy library without each campus drafting independently.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A policy drafting tool where the compliance officer inputs what changed (e.g., 'update financial aid policy for 2025 CHED guidelines') and AI generates a revised policy draft in the organization's standard template, ready for approval and central distribution.

matches.match_rationale: The full policy governance workflow — ingest documents from campus drives, detect version mismatches, notify campus managers of required updates, confirm acknowledgement, and update the audit trail — is a multi-step workflow ideal for task automation. No single AI capability covers the end-to-end loop; an agent can orchestrate extraction, comparison, notification, and logging across systems (SharePoint, email, policy registry) to enforce centralized control without manual coordination.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A policy compliance agent that automatically scans campus document repositories on a schedule, compares versions against master, sends update notifications to campus managers, tracks acknowledgements, and generates a compliance dashboard for DepEd/accreditor audit readiness.
